description: Streptavidin-binding peptide (SBP-Tag) — a 38-aa synthetic peptide selected by mRNA display that binds streptavidin with nanomolar affinity (Kd ~2.5 nM) for mild, single-step affinity purification and detection, eluted competitively with biotin.

references.authors: Wilson DS, Keefe AD, Szostak JW
references.title: The use of mRNA display to select high-affinity protein-binding peptides

references.authors: Keefe AD, Wilson DS, Seelig B, Szostak JW
references.title: One-step purification of recombinant proteins using a nanomolar-affinity streptavidin-binding peptide, the SBP-Tag

references.authors: Barrette-Ng IH, Wu SC, Tjia WM, Wong SL, Ng KKS
references.title: The structure of the SBP-Tag-streptavidin complex reveals a novel helical scaffold bridging binding pockets on separate subunits

references.authors: Kim JH, Chang TM, Graham AN, Choo KHA, Kalitsis P, Hudson DF
references.title: Streptavidin-Binding Peptide (SBP)-tagged SMC2 allows single-step affinity fluorescence, blotting or purification of the condensin complex

references.authors: Burckstummer T, Bennett KL, Preradovic A, et al.
references.title: An efficient tandem affinity purification procedure for interaction proteomics in mammalian cells
Single-Step Affinity Purification of ERK Signaling Complexes Using the Streptavidin-Binding Peptide (SBP) Tag

label: SBP_tag
note: Streptavidin-binding peptide (SBP-Tag), 38 aa (MDEKTTGWRGGHVVEGLAGELEQLRARLEHHPQGQREP). Synthetic peptide isolated by mRNA display that binds streptavidin with Kd ~2.5 nM; used for one-step affinity purification with mild competitive elution by biotin. Verified to 100% identity over all 38 residues against PDB 4JO6 chain Z. The peptide binds bivalently, contacting the biotin-binding pockets of two separate streptavidin subunits via an N-terminal HVV motif (residues 12-14) and a C-terminal HPQ motif (residues 31-33) bridged by a central alpha-helix (residues 17-28).

provenance.sequence_source: 4JO6_Z (PDB/NCBI protein, Chain Z "SBP-Tag", deposited 2013-03-17), 100% identity over 38 aa (full length); cross-checked against AAK55118.1 (pTAG2K fusion, Keefe et al. 2001, PMID 11722181) which embeds the same 38-aa SBP sequence at residues 373-410

functional_claims.label: The SBP-Tag binds streptavidin with an equilibrium dissociation constant of about 2.5 nM.

source.quote: The tag binds to streptavidin with an equilibrium dissociation constant of 2.5 nM.

functional_claims.label: SBP-tagged proteins are purified in a single step on immobilised streptavidin and eluted under very mild, specific conditions with biotin in the wash buffer; the matrix capacity is about 0.5 mg of tagged protein per mL.

source.quote: The elution conditions from the streptavidin column are very mild and specific, consisting of the wash buffer plus biotin... The capacity of the immobilized streptavidin used to purify SBP-tagged proteins is about 0.5 mg per milliliter of matrix.

functional_claims.label: A single-step purification of SBP-tagged proteins from bacterial extract yields preparations of higher purity than maltose-binding-protein (MBP) or His-tag purification, suiting it to high-throughput protein purification.

value.comparison: purer than MBP- or His-tag single-step purification

source.quote: A single-step purification of SBP-tagged proteins from bacterial extract yields samples that are more pure than those purified using maltose-binding protein or the His-tag.

functional_claims.label: The 38-aa peptide binds streptavidin bivalently, simultaneously occupying biotin-binding pockets on two separate streptavidin subunits; an N-terminal HVV motif (residues 12-14) and a C-terminal HPQ motif (residues 31-33) each engage a pocket, bridged by a central alpha-helix (residues 17-28) that projects three leucine side chains into a groove between protomers.

value.mechanism: bivalent binding to two streptavidin subunits

source.quote: The structure of the SBP-Tag-streptavidin complex reveals a novel helical scaffold bridging binding pockets on separate subunits... simultaneously interacting with biotin-binding pockets from two separate subunits... N-terminal HVV sequence at residues 12-14... C-terminal HPQ sequence at residues 31-33... residues 17-28 adopt an alpha-helical structure projecting three leucine side chains.

functional_claims.label: A minimal variant, SBP-Tag2 (residues 11-34 of the full SBP-Tag, 24-25 aa), retains streptavidin binding comparable to the full-length tag; terminal residues 1-10 and 35-38 do not contact streptavidin and are dispensable.

value.binding: SBP-Tag2 binding affinity comparable to full-length

source.quote: Residues 1-10 and 35-38 of the original SBP-Tag... do not appear to contact streptavidin... SBP-Tag2 minimal variant residue range 11-34... binding affinity comparable to full-length SBP-Tag.

functional_claims.label: The SBP-Tag derives from a streptavidin-binding peptide selected from a large random peptide library by mRNA display (lead clone SB19, Kd ~10 nM; deletion-refined high-affinity derivative SB19-C4, Kd ~2.4 nM); the 38-aa tag was defined from this lineage.

value.method: mRNA display selection from a random peptide library

source.quote: high-affinity aptamers can be obtained even from random nonconstrained peptide libraries... SB19... KD = 10 +/- 1.8 nM... SB19-C4-FLAG... a KD of 2.4 nM.

functional_claims.label: The small (4 kDa) peptide functions as an N-terminal, C-terminal, or internal fusion across bacterial and mammalian systems, supporting single-step purification, streptavidin-conjugate fluorescence imaging and immunoblot detection of recombinant proteins and endogenous protein complexes.

source.quote: Streptavidin-Binding Peptide (SBP)-tagged SMC2 allows single-step affinity fluorescence, blotting or purification of the condensin complex
